text: Risk management is a key skill in both business and everyday life. It begins with identifying risks—spotting potential problems before they happen. Once risks are clear, you can develop mitigation strategies to reduce their impact, such as improving safety procedures or buying insurance. However, even with good planning, some risks may still occur. This is why having contingency plans is important: they provide a backup course of action if things go wrong. Finally, effective risk management requires ongoing monitoring. Risks can change over time, so regularly reviewing your plans helps you stay prepared and adjust your approach as needed. By following these steps, you can handle challenges with greater confidence.
